name: accela-mcp
description: MCP server for the Accela Construct API — search records, inspections, fees, documents, and more.
long_description:
Connects Claude Desktop to your Accela tenant. Search permits, look up parcels and contacts, list inspections, read fees and documents — all from chat.

## After installing

1. **Get your Accela app credentials.** On the [Accela Developer Portal](https://developer.accela.com), create an app and copy its App ID and App Secret. Use the redirect URI `http://localhost:8765/oauth/callback` (or pick your own and configure it on both sides).
2. **Fill in the Configuration tab below.** App ID, App Secret, Agency, Environment, and Redirect URI are required. Leave **Encryption Key** blank — the server generates one on first run and persists it locally.
3. **Pick which Accela tools you want.** Read tools register by default. The host's Tool Permissions panel lets you allow / ask / deny each one individually. To enable write tools, flip **Allow Write Tools** below — that registers all write tools so they show up in Tool Permissions.
4. **Log in from chat.** Once installed, ask Claude:  *“Log me into Accela.”* Claude will call the `accela_login` tool, open your browser to Accela's authorize page, capture the redirect, and save tokens.
5. **Restart Claude Desktop** so the rest of the Accela tools become visible.

## In-chat tools that don't need login

- `accela_auth_status` — check whether you're logged in and when your token expires.
- `accela_login` — start (or repeat) the OAuth login.

If an Accela API call later returns 401, the response includes a hint pointing back at `accela_login`.

app_id.title: Accela App ID
app_id.description: Connection — From your app on the Accela Developer Portal (https://developer.accela.com).

app_secret.title: Accela App Secret
app_secret.description: Connection — From the same app on the Accela Developer Portal. Keep this private.

agency.title: Agency
agency.description: Connection — Your Accela agency name, e.g. NULLISLAND for the public sandbox or your jurisdiction's identifier for production.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Connection — Tenant environment, typically TEST, STAGE, or PROD. Match what's registered on the Developer Portal.

redirect_uri.title: OAuth Redirect URI
redirect_uri.description: Connection — Must match the redirect URI registered on your Accela app. Defaults to a loopback URL on port 8765.

mcp_key.title: Encryption Key (advanced)
mcp_key.description: Connection — Fernet key used to encrypt the local token file. Leave blank; the server generates one on first run and persists it under your platform's user config directory.

writes_enabled.title: Allow Write Tools
writes_enabled.description: Master switch. Off by default. Flip on to register every write tool (create / update / cancel records, schedule inspections, upload documents, etc.) and let confirmed calls actually mutate Accela data. The host's Tool Permissions panel still gates each individual write tool. Restart the host after toggling to refresh the tool list.

payments_real_money_allowed.title: Allow Real-Money Payments
payments_real_money_allowed.description: Extra gate on top of Allow Write Tools for the Payments — Write group's commit step. Off by default. Production environments require an additional flag in capabilities.yaml.
